points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Meeting called to order at 6:03 PM with roll call completed for committee members
points.source: Jesse Cupp : So this is chair Jessica on the meeting to order at six O 3:00 PM And let's go to roll call committee member Cliff Grant.

topics.topic: Public Art Inventory and Mapping Progress

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Three additional art pieces found at El Camino Hospital through Sunnyvale's mapping website
points.source: Susi Merhar : I found on a different, I think it was City of Sunnyvale's kind of map that they're using. I looked at Mountain View on that map website and there were three more art pieces that were all in El Camino Hospital

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Next steps: John Lang will work with the City's GIS team to update the online map using the spreadsheet, explore modern ArcGIS StoryMaps and trail overlays, and consider an intake process for future updates. The current public map was last updated in 2016; publication is planned after updates.
points.source: John Lang : So I think what I I see personally as a next step this can continue to be a living document and will be a living document means to but I think taking what what has been generated by the subcommittee and i'm. Happy to kind of talk with our GIS team

points.speaker: Cliff Grant
points.text: Scope considerations include whether interior works (e.g., hospital hallways) are considered public art and coordinating with El Camino Hospital, which did not respond to public inquiry. The City will engage institutions directly before publishing interior works and clarify inclusion criteria.
points.source: Cliff Grant : I was walking through El Camino, taking photos of the glass sculptures that we had on the list, and I stumbled over a statue in the courtyard.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Strategy includes vision statement: 'Creation of diverse public art enriches Mountain View's innovative and creative culture, enhances community vibrancy, supports local artists and transforms Mountain View'
points.source: John Lang : The creation of diverse public art enriches mountain views innovative and creative culture, enhances the vibrancy of our community, supports local artists and transforms Mountain View into the North. That's the vision.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Three main strategies identified: develop city policies for art appreciation, create more public art opportunities, and pursue collaboration with artist organizations
points.source: John Lang : So the three strategies are develop, augment, and refined city policies and procedures to better line and encourage art appreciation and placekeeping, create more public art opportunities, providing for public expression and vitality of art in the Mountain View community, and pursue new opportunities for collaboration with artist organizations.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Suggested initiative clarifications include: explicitly treating private percent-for-art; improving visibility via guided tours and the online map; recognizing trails, easements, and rights-of-way in siting; enabling interactive and temporary art events; and including public transit routes and a maintenance plan in the utility box program.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I thought it would. Be a couple other things in there. So prioritizes public art and parks, open spaces, pedestrian and bike trails, right of ways and other areas.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Funding flexibility will be explored, such as whether K5 (public project) art funds can be pooled or reallocated to higher-visibility locations, while noting in-lieu payments on private projects already create a flexible pool. Legal and policy feasibility will be vetted before codifying.
points.source: John Lang : So I put it here because I don't know the answer. Like this is good. Figure it out and see if there's a way to make it work.

points.speaker: Cliff Grant
points.text: Next step agreed: the ad hoc committee will draft revised goals (and consider any vision alignment) for full committee review in September, while staff incorporates feedback and feasibility checks. This approach aims to accelerate revisions by giving the committee a concrete draft to refine.
points.source: Cliff Grant : So, so, so the plan would be for this the ad hoc committee work on a draft we'll bring. We'll bring back then. And then we'll review it in September.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Given the heavy September workload, the committee may consider an August meeting or two September meetings. John Lang acknowledged the need to keep meetings functional and will explore options, but no scheduling decision was made at this time.
points.source: John Lang : Let's put a pin in that. Happy to explore that. But let's put a pin in that, given not quite the right section of the agenda.

topics.topic: Economic Impact Research for Strategy Support

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff will pair city event attendance data with conservative AEP metrics to communicate arts' value (e.g., Art & Wine Festival, performances) and support adoption of the Public Art Strategy, acknowledging updated AEP6 data will refine estimates when released.
points.source: John Lang : So there is a way we can kind of plug this in and even just being conservative, we're going to use 2017 numbers because that was the last number it was and just say, yeah, this is the number you we're. Going to get an estimate.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: John Lang explained chat is disabled for decision-making meetings by City policy.
points.source: John Lang : The chat is disabled, but I believe I have your e-mail. I'll send you my contact information, but we should probably talk and so we can better capture.

decisions.assignee: Visual Arts Committee Ad Hoc Committee
decisions.text: Draft revised public art goals (and consider vision alignment) for full committee discussion by the September meeting
decisions.source: Cliff Grant : So, so, so the plan would be for this the ad hoc committee work on a draft we'll bring. We'll bring back then. And then we'll review it in September.

decisions.text: Coordinate with the GIS team to update the online public art map using the new inventory, explore StoryMaps and overlays, and prepare an updated public-facing map
decisions.source: John Lang : So I think what I I see personally as a next step this can continue to be a living document and will be a living document means to but I think taking what what has been generated by the subcommittee and i'm. Happy to kind of talk with our GIS team

decisions.text: Pre-screen transit grade separation art submissions, then circulate to the committee for scoring and discussion at the September meeting
decisions.source: John Lang : So my intent is to have a public works review at first just initial screen in anticipation of probably having questions from from the VAC on this. So I'll ask them to screen it first. And then out to the committee.

decisions.text: Proceed with the 2024 CPA call for artists without a theme and return in October to finalize selections and align future calls to the summer schedule
decisions.source: John Lang : So the idea is we'll come back, We'll add it to September for October, but like. Come back in October to to finalize that and then we'll get on schedule and then it'll be in basically the June timeframe and 2024 you'll do this again.

decisions.text: Implement the approved annual work plan change to emphasize promoting the Public Art Strategy, including updating the 1996 art book, online map, and website
decisions.source: Jesse Cupp : I will make a motion to approve the work plan with that change... It passes unanimously, OK.

decisions.text: Email Jasmin Aber to arrange an offline discussion on VAC processes and engagement
decisions.source: John Lang : The chat is disabled, but I believe I have your e-mail. I'll send you my contact information, but we should probably talk and so we can better capture.

decisions.text: Staff will research feasibility of moving K5 policy funds between projects and creating alternative funding mechanisms
decisions.source: John Lang : So I put it here because I don't know the answer. Like this is good. Figure it out and see if there's a way to make it work.

decisions.text: Staff will work with GIS team to update public art map with new inventory data and explore enhanced functionality
decisions.source: John Lang : So I think what I I see personally as a next step this can continue to be a living document and will be a living document means to but I think taking what what has been generated by the subcommittee and i'm. Happy to kind of talk with our GIS team
